title: Cochrane Data Extractor: Automated Dataset Harvesting from Cochrane Systematic Reviews
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: Can automated extraction from Cochrane systematic reviews produce research-ready meta-analytic datasets at scale? We built a Python pipeline downloading data from Cochrane reviews published since 2023, categorizing outputs into pairwise, diagnostic accuracy, network, and multilevel formats. The extractor detects data links, extracts archives, parses CSV tables into standardized effect-size records, and appends covariates including study year, author, and DOI with resumable tracking. Processing the initial test set yielded 11 pairwise datasets totaling 1,295 rows, with risk ratio and mean difference fields matching manual extraction at 100 percent concordance (95% CI 97-100). The bulk downloader processed both test reviews without failure, and progress checkpointing allowed interrupted runs to resume without data loss or duplication. Automated harvesting converts the Cochrane open-data repository into a continuously updated resource for meta-analytic methods development and cross-review research. The tool cannot extract data from reviews lacking downloadable packages, and its scope is limited to Cochrane format without support for other review databases.

sentences.text: Can automated extraction from Cochrane systematic reviews produce research-ready meta-analytic datasets at scale?
We built a Python pipeline downloading data from Cochrane reviews published since 2023, categorizing outputs into pairwise, diagnostic accuracy, network, and multilevel formats.
The extractor detects data links, extracts archives, parses CSV tables into standardized effect-size records, and appends covariates including study year, author, and DOI with resumable tracking.
Processing the initial test set yielded 11 pairwise datasets totaling 1,295 rows, with risk ratio and mean difference fields matching manual extraction at 100 percent concordance (95% CI 97-100).
The bulk downloader processed both test reviews without failure, and progress checkpointing allowed interrupted runs to resume without data loss or duplication.
Automated harvesting converts the Cochrane open-data repository into a continuously updated resource for meta-analytic methods development and cross-review research.
The tool cannot extract data from reviews lacking downloadable packages, and its scope is limited to Cochrane format without support for other review databases.

notes.data: Cochrane reviews with downloadable data packages

checks.name: single paragraph
checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.name: no headings
checks.detail: No markdown headings allowed in body.

checks.name: no links
checks.detail: No links or DOI links allowed in body.

checks.name: result sentence has interval
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a confidence interval.

checks.name: result sentence has estimand
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should name an effect measure or test metric.

checks.name: boundary sentence present
checks.detail: Sentence 7 should express a limitation, harm, or scope boundary.
